title: Meat Without Mirth.
content:
Eaten I have; and though I had good cheer,
    I did not sup, because no friends were there.
    Where mirth and friends are absent when we dine
    Or sup, there wants the incense and the wine.
meaning:
"Meat Without Mirth." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
